paper.title: Ancient, eclipsing, tidally-locked: A blue lurker progenitor in the population of extreme-velocity star candidates

review.summary: Four internal tables: best-fit atmospheric parameters from X-shooter spectroscopy (tab:params1), median light-curve parameters of the eclipsing binary (tab:lc_parameters), combined spectro-photometric parameters including distances and tangential velocities (tab:params2), and radial-velocity measurements from three instruments (Tab:compspec). Three external resources retained: SOAR/Goodman and NOT/ALFOSC instrument pages, and the SOAR_data_reducer GitHub repository used for spectral reduction.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Best-fit atmospheric and kinematic parameters for the two metal-poor stars (Gaia-S and WISE J0751) derived from X-shooter spectroscopy via chi-squared minimisation.

source_refs.caption: Best fit parameters for X-shooter spectrum fitted using a $\chi^2$ minimization method. Statistical uncertainties are provided.
source_refs.label: tab:params1

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the atmospheric or kinematic parameter

internal_tables.evidence: Best fit parameters for X-shooter spectrum fitted using a $\chi^2$ minimization method. Statistical uncertainties are provided.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Median light-curve parameters of the Gaia-S eclipsing binary system derived from ZTF photometry, with 1-sigma uncertainties.

source_refs.caption: Median light curve Parameters with $1\sigma$ uncertanties
source_refs.label: tab:lc_parameters

internal_tables.evidence: Median light curve Parameters with $1\sigma$ uncertanties

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Combined spectro-photometric parameters for both stars, including angular diameters, reddening, evolutionary masses, radii, spectroscopic distances, tangential velocities, and luminosities from SED fitting.

source_refs.caption: The combined spectro-photometric parameters for the two stars.
source_refs.label: tab:params2

internal_tables.evidence: The combined spectro-photometric parameters for the two stars.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Radial-velocity measurements of Gaia-S from three different spectrographs (X-shooter, NOT/ALFOSC, SOAR/Goodman), used to confirm the large RV shift and binary nature.

source_refs.caption: Radial velocity measurements from different instruments. Uncertainties are systematic.
source_refs.label: Tab:compspec

columns.name: MJD
columns.meaning: Modified Julian Date of the observation

internal_tables.evidence: Radial velocity measurements from different instruments. Uncertainties are systematic.

external_resources.description: SOAR telescope Goodman spectrograph instrument page.

source_refs.context:
A precise measurement of the atmospheric parameters, metallicity, and radial velocity was required to probe further. The star was later reported to be variable in \citet{Gaia3}, although with only a few data points.

We obtained optical spectra at the ESO VLT with the X-shooter instrument at high signal-to-noise ($\mathrm{S/N}>60$) in the blue and VIS arms of X-shooter ($\lambda = 3000 - 9000\, \text{\AA}$ at a resolving power $R = 5500 \,\text{to}\, 11500$). Due to the light curve variability in the Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) light curve, two low S/N, low resolution spectra were obtained to check for radial velocity (RV) variability from the Nordic Optical Telescope (NOT/ALFOSC)\footnote{https://www.not.iac.es/instruments/alfosc/} and Southern Astrophysical Research (SOAR/Goodman)\footnote{https://noirlab.edu/public/programs/ctio/soar-telescope/goodman/} spectrographs  covering the optical range from approximately 3600 \,\AA\, to 5200 \AA. Using a 1" slit, each setup achieves a spectral resolution of 2.2\,\AA, and 2.6\,\AA, respectively, corresponding to RV precisions between 10 and 15 \kms. 
For \wise\, we reanalyzed an archival X-Shooter spectrum ($R$ $= 6655$ in the blue arm, and $R$ $=8935$ in the VIS arm), of high S/N ($165$ in the blue, $178$ in the VIS).

external_resources.description: Open-source Python pipeline used for bias subtraction, flat-field correction, and wavelength calibration of SOAR/Goodman and NOT/ALFOSC spectra.
